lines.gurmukhi: ਇਉ ਗੁਰਮੁਖਿ ਆਪੁ ਨਿਵਾਰੀਐ ਸਭੁ ਰਾਜੁ ਸ੍ਰਿਸਟਿ ਕਾ ਲੇਇ ॥
lines.translit: eiau guramukh aap nivaareeai sabh raaj srisatt kaa lee |
lines.english: This is how the Gurmukhs eliminate their self-conceit, and come to rule the whole world.

lines.gurmukhi: ਨਾਨਕ ਗੁਰਮੁਖਿ ਬੁਝੀਐ ਜਾ ਆਪੇ ਨਦਰਿ ਕਰੇਇ ॥੧॥
lines.translit: naanak guramukh bujheeai jaa aape nadar karee |1|
lines.english: O Nanak, the Gurmukh understands, when the Lord casts His Glance of Grace. ||1||

lines.gurmukhi: ਜਿਨ ਗੁਰਮੁਖਿ ਨਾਮੁ ਧਿਆਇਆ ਆਏ ਤੇ ਪਰਵਾਣੁ ॥
lines.translit: jin guramukh naam dhiaaeaa aae te paravaan |
lines.english: Blessed and approved is the coming into the world, of those Gurmukhs who meditate on the Naam, the Name of the Lord.

lines.translit: mahalaa 3 |
lines.english: Third Mehl:

lines.gurmukhi: ਨਾਨਕ ਕੁਲ ਉਧਾਰਹਿ ਆਪਣਾ ਦਰਗਹ ਪਾਵਹਿ ਮਾਣੁ ॥੨॥
lines.translit: naanak kul udhaareh aapanaa daragah paaveh maan |2|
lines.english: O Nanak, they save their families, and they are honored in the Court of the Lord. ||2||

lines.gurmukhi: ਪਉੜੀ ॥
lines.translit: paurree |
lines.english: Pauree:

lines.gurmukhi: ਗੁਰਮੁਖਿ ਸਖੀਆ ਸਿਖ ਗੁਰੂ ਮੇਲਾਈਆ ॥
lines.translit: guramukh sakheea sikh guroo melaaeea |
lines.english: The Guru unites His Sikhs, the Gurmukhs, with the Lord.

lines.gurmukhi: ਇਕਿ ਸੇਵਕ ਗੁਰ ਪਾਸਿ ਇਕਿ ਗੁਰਿ ਕਾਰੈ ਲਾਈਆ ॥
lines.translit: eik sevak gur paas ik gur kaarai laaeea |
lines.english: The Guru keeps some of them with Himself, and engages others in His Service.

lines.gurmukhi: ਜਿਨਾ ਗੁਰੁ ਪਿਆਰਾ ਮਨਿ ਚਿਤਿ ਤਿਨਾ ਭਾਉ ਗੁਰੂ ਦੇਵਾਈਆ ॥
lines.translit: jinaa gur piaaraa man chit tinaa bhaau guroo devaaeea |
lines.english: Those who cherish their Beloved in their conscious minds, the Guru blesses them with His Love.

lines.gurmukhi: ਗੁਰ ਸਿਖਾ ਇਕੋ ਪਿਆਰੁ ਗੁਰ ਮਿਤਾ ਪੁਤਾ ਭਾਈਆ ॥
lines.translit: gur sikhaa iko piaar gur mitaa putaa bhaaeea |
lines.english: The Guru loves all of His Gursikhs equally well, like friends, children and siblings.

lines.gurmukhi: ਗੁਰੁ ਸਤਿਗੁਰੁ ਬੋਲਹੁ ਸਭਿ ਗੁਰੁ ਆਖਿ ਗੁਰੂ ਜੀਵਾਈਆ ॥੧੪॥
lines.translit: gur satigur bolahu sabh gur aakh guroo jeevaaeea |14|
lines.english: So chant the Name of the Guru, the True Guru, everyone! Chanting the Name of the Guru, Guru, you shall be rejuvenated. ||14||

lines.gurmukhi: ਨਾਨਕ ਨਾਮੁ ਨ ਚੇਤਨੀ ਅਗਿਆਨੀ ਅੰਧੁਲੇ ਅਵਰੇ ਕਰਮ ਕਮਾਹਿ ॥
lines.translit: naanak naam na chetanee agiaanee andhule avare karam kamaeh |
lines.english: O Nanak, the blind, ignorant fools do not remember the Naam, the Name of the Lord; they involve themselves in other activities.

lines.gurmukhi: ਸਲੋਕੁ ਮਃ ੩ ॥
lines.translit: salok mahalaa 3 |
lines.english: Salok, Third Mehl:

lines.gurmukhi: ਨਾਨਕ ਸਤਿਗੁਰੁ ਸੇਵਹਿ ਆਪਣਾ ਸੇ ਜਨ ਸਚੇ ਪਰਵਾਣੁ ॥
lines.translit: naanak satigur seveh aapanaa se jan sache paravaan |
lines.english: O Nanak, those humble beings are true and approved, who serve their True Guru.

lines.gurmukhi: ਜਮ ਦਰਿ ਬਧੇ ਮਾਰੀਅਹਿ ਫਿਰਿ ਵਿਸਟਾ ਮਾਹਿ ਪਚਾਹਿ ॥੧॥
lines.translit: jam dar badhe maareeeh fir visattaa maeh pachaeh |1|
lines.english: They are bound and gagged at the door of the Messenger of Death; they are punished, and in the end, they rot away in manure. ||1||

lines.translit: mahalaa 3 |
lines.english: Third Mehl:

lines.gurmukhi: ਪਉੜੀ ॥
lines.translit: paurree |
lines.english: Pauree:

lines.gurmukhi: ਹਰਿ ਕੈ ਨਾਇ ਸਮਾਇ ਰਹੇ ਚੂਕਾ ਆਵਣੁ ਜਾਣੁ ॥੨॥
lines.translit: har kai naae samaae rahe chookaa aavan jaan |2|
lines.english: They remain absorbed in the Name of the Lord, and their comings and goings cease. ||2||

lines.gurmukhi: ਧਨੁ ਸੰਪੈ ਮਾਇਆ ਸੰਚੀਐ ਅੰਤੇ ਦੁਖਦਾਈ ॥
lines.translit: dhan sanpai maaeaa sancheeai ante dukhadaaee |
lines.english: Gathering the wealth and property of Maya, brings only pain in the end.

lines.gurmukhi: ਹਰ ਰੰਗੀ ਤੁਰੇ ਨਿਤ ਪਾਲੀਅਹਿ ਕਿਤੈ ਕਾਮਿ ਨ ਆਈ ॥
lines.translit: har rangee ture nit paaleeeh kitai kaam na aaee |
lines.english: He may breed horses of various colors, but these will not be of any use to him.

lines.gurmukhi: ਘਰ ਮੰਦਰ ਮਹਲ ਸਵਾਰੀਅਹਿ ਕਿਛੁ ਸਾਥਿ ਨ ਜਾਈ ॥
lines.translit: ghar mandar mehal savaareeeh kichh saath na jaaee |
lines.english: Homes, mansions and adorned palaces will not go with anyone.

lines.gurmukhi: ਜਨ ਲਾਵਹੁ ਚਿਤੁ ਹਰਿ ਨਾਮ ਸਿਉ ਅੰਤਿ ਹੋਇ ਸਖਾਈ ॥
lines.translit: jan laavahu chit har naam siau ant hoe sakhaaee |
lines.english: O human, link your consciousness to the Lord's Name, and in the end, it shall be your companion and helper.

lines.gurmukhi: ਜਨ ਨਾਨਕ ਨਾਮੁ ਧਿਆਇਆ ਗੁਰਮੁਖਿ ਸੁਖੁ ਪਾਈ ॥੧੫॥
lines.translit: jan naanak naam dhiaaeaa guramukh sukh paaee |15|
lines.english: Servant Nanak meditates on the Naam, the Name of the Lord; the Gurmukh is blessed with peace. ||15||

lines.gurmukhi: ਬਿਨੁ ਕਰਮੈ ਨਾਉ ਨ ਪਾਈਐ ਪੂਰੈ ਕਰਮਿ ਪਾਇਆ ਜਾਇ ॥
lines.translit: bin karamai naau na paaeeai poorai karam paaeaa jaae |
lines.english: Without the karma of good actions, the Name is not obtained; it can be obtained only by perfect good karma.

lines.gurmukhi: ਸਲੋਕੁ ਮਃ ੩ ॥
lines.translit: salok mahalaa 3 |
lines.english: Salok, Third Mehl:

lines.gurmukhi: ਨਾਨਕ ਨਦਰਿ ਕਰੇ ਜੇ ਆਪਣੀ ਤਾ ਗੁਰਮਤਿ ਮੇਲਿ ਮਿਲਾਇ ॥੧॥
lines.translit: naanak nadar kare je aapanee taa guramat mel milaae |1|
lines.english: O Nanak, if the Lord casts His Glance of Grace, then under Guru's Instruction, one is united in His Union. ||1||

lines.gurmukhi: ਇਕ ਦਝਹਿ ਇਕ ਦਬੀਅਹਿ ਇਕਨਾ ਕੁਤੇ ਖਾਹਿ ॥
lines.translit: eik dajheh ik dabeeeh ikanaa kute khaeh |
lines.english: Some are cremated, and some are buried; some are eaten by dogs.

lines.translit: mahalaa 1 |
lines.english: First Mehl:

lines.gurmukhi: ਇਕਿ ਪਾਣੀ ਵਿਚਿ ਉਸਟੀਅਹਿ ਇਕਿ ਭੀ ਫਿਰਿ ਹਸਣਿ ਪਾਹਿ ॥
lines.translit: eik paanee vich usatteeeh ik bhee fir hasan paeh |
lines.english: Some are thrown into water, while others are thrown into wells.

lines.gurmukhi: ਪਉੜੀ ॥
lines.translit: paurree |
lines.english: Pauree:

lines.gurmukhi: ਤਿਨ ਕਾ ਖਾਧਾ ਪੈਧਾ ਮਾਇਆ ਸਭੁ ਪਵਿਤੁ ਹੈ ਜੋ ਨਾਮਿ ਹਰਿ ਰਾਤੇ ॥
lines.translit: tin kaa khaadhaa paidhaa maaeaa sabh pavit hai jo naam har raate |
lines.english: The food and clothes, and all the worldly possessions of those who are attuned to the Lord's Name are sacred.

lines.gurmukhi: ਨਾਨਕ ਏਵ ਨ ਜਾਪਈ ਕਿਥੈ ਜਾਇ ਸਮਾਹਿ ॥੨॥
lines.translit: naanak ev na jaapee kithai jaae samaeh |2|
lines.english: O Nanak, it is not known, where they go and into what they merge. ||2||

lines.gurmukhi: ਤਿਨ ਕੇ ਘਰ ਮੰਦਰ ਮਹਲ ਸਰਾਈ ਸਭਿ ਪਵਿਤੁ ਹਹਿ ਜਿਨੀ ਗੁਰਮੁਖਿ ਸੇਵਕ ਸਿਖ ਅਭਿਆਗਤ ਜਾਇ ਵਰਸਾਤੇ ॥
lines.translit: tin ke ghar mandar mehal saraaee sabh pavit heh jinee guramukh sevak sikh abhiaagat jaae varasaate |
lines.english: All the homes, temples, palaces and way-stations are sacred, where the Gurmukhs, the selfless servants, the Sikhs and the renouncers of the world, go and take their rest.

lines.gurmukhi: ਤਿਨ ਕੇ ਤੁਰੇ ਜੀਨ ਖੁਰਗੀਰ ਸਭਿ ਪਵਿਤੁ ਹਹਿ ਜਿਨੀ ਗੁਰਮੁਖਿ ਸਿਖ ਸਾਧ ਸੰਤ ਚੜਿ ਜਾਤੇ ॥
lines.translit: tin ke ture jeen khurageer sabh pavit heh jinee guramukh sikh saadh sant charr jaate |
lines.english: All the horses, saddles and horse blankets are sacred, upon which the Gurmukhs, the Sikhs, the Holy and the Saints, mount and ride.

lines.gurmukhi: ਤਿਨ ਕੇ ਕਰਮ ਧਰਮ ਕਾਰਜ ਸਭਿ ਪਵਿਤੁ ਹਹਿ ਜੋ ਬੋਲਹਿ ਹਰਿ ਹਰਿ ਰਾਮ ਨਾਮੁ ਹਰਿ ਸਾਤੇ ॥
lines.translit: tin ke karam dharam kaaraj sabh pavit heh jo boleh har har raam naam har saate |
lines.english: All the rituals and Dharmic practices and deeds are sacred, for those who utter the Name of the Lord, Har, Har, the True Name of the Lord.

lines.gurmukhi: ਜਿਨ ਕੈ ਪੋਤੈ ਪੁੰਨੁ ਹੈ ਸੇ ਗੁਰਮੁਖਿ ਸਿਖ ਗੁਰੂ ਪਹਿ ਜਾਤੇ ॥੧੬॥
lines.translit: jin kai potai pun hai se guramukh sikh guroo peh jaate |16|
lines.english: Those Gurmukhs, those Sikhs, who have purity as their treasure, go to their Guru. ||16||

lines.gurmukhi: ਸਲੋਕੁ ਮਃ ੩ ॥
lines.translit: salok mahalaa 3 |
lines.english: Salok, Third Mehl:

lines.gurmukhi: ਨਾਨਕ ਨਾਵਹੁ ਘੁਥਿਆ ਹਲਤੁ ਪਲਤੁ ਸਭੁ ਜਾਇ ॥
lines.translit: naanak naavahu ghuthiaa halat palat sabh jaae |
lines.english: O Nanak, forsaking the Name, he loses everything, in this world and the next.

lines.gurmukhi: ਜਪੁ ਤਪੁ ਸੰਜਮੁ ਸਭੁ ਹਿਰਿ ਲਇਆ ਮੁਠੀ ਦੂਜੈ ਭਾਇ ॥
lines.translit: jap tap sanjam sabh hir leaa mutthee doojai bhaae |
lines.english: Chanting, deep meditation and austere self-disciplined practices are all wasted; he is deceived by the love of duality.

lines.gurmukhi: ਜਮ ਦਰਿ ਬਧੇ ਮਾਰੀਅਹਿ ਬਹੁਤੀ ਮਿਲੈ ਸਜਾਇ ॥੧॥
lines.translit: jam dar badhe maareeeh bahutee milai sajaae |1|
lines.english: He is bound and gagged at the door of the Messenger of Death. He is beaten, and receives terrible punishment. ||1||
